info.name: API Documentation
info.description:
StartFragment

# Postman API Documentation

## Base URL

``` txt
http://localhost:5000

 ```

---

# Authentication APIs

Base Path:

``` txt
/api/v1/auth

 ```

## 1\. Register User

### Endpoint

``` http
POST /api/v1/auth/register

 ```

### Description

Creates a new user account.

### Request Headers

| Key | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Content-Type | application/json |

### Request Body

``` json
{
  "name": "John Doe",
  "email": "john@example.com",
  "password": "123456"
}

 ```

### Success Response

``` json
{
  "_id": "664f1e4a2f6f9d0012345678",
  "name": "John Doe",
  "email": "john@example.com",
  "token": "JWT_TOKEN"
}

 ```

---

## 2\. Login User

### Endpoint

``` http
POST /api/v1/auth/login

 ```

### Description

Authenticates a user and returns a JWT token.

### Request Headers

| Key | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Content-Type | application/json |

### Request Body

``` json
{
  "email": "john@example.com",
  "password": "123456"
}

 ```

### Success Response

``` json
{
  "_id": "664f1e4a2f6f9d0012345678",
  "name": "John Doe",
  "email": "john@example.com",
  "token": "JWT_TOKEN"
}

 ```

---

## 3\. Protected Route

### Endpoint

``` http
GET /api/v1/auth/protected

 ```

### Description

Returns authenticated user information.

### Authorization

Bearer Token Required

### Request Headers

| Key | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Authorization | Bearer JWT_TOKEN |

### Success Response

``` json
{
  "msg": "Protected route",
  "user": {
    "id": "664f1e4a2f6f9d0012345678",
    "email": "john@example.com"
  }
}

 ```

---

# Product APIs

Base Path:

``` txt
/api/products

 ```

All product APIs require JWT authentication.

---

## 4\. Create Product

### Endpoint

``` http
POST /api/products

 ```

### Authorization

Bearer Token Required

### Request Headers

| Key | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Content-Type | application/json |
| Authorization | Bearer JWT_TOKEN |

### Request Body

``` json
{
  "name": "Laptop",
  "description": "Gaming Laptop",
  "price": 75000,
  "image": "https://example.com/laptop.jpg"
}

 ```

### Success Response

``` json
{
  "_id": "665000000000000000000001",
  "name": "Laptop",
  "description": "Gaming Laptop",
  "price": 75000,
  "image": "https://example.com/laptop.jpg"
}

 ```

---

## 5\. Get All Products

### Endpoint

``` http
GET /api/products

 ```

### Authorization

Bearer Token Required

### Request Headers

| Key | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Authorization | Bearer JWT_TOKEN |

### Success Response

``` json
[
  {
    "_id": "665000000000000000000001",
    "name": "Laptop",
    "description": "Gaming Laptop",
    "price": 75000,
    "image": "https://example.com/laptop.jpg"
  }
]

 ```

---

## 6\. Get Single Product

### Endpoint

``` http
GET /api/products/:id

 ```

### Example

``` http
GET /api/products/665000000000000000000001

 ```

### Authorization

Bearer Token Required

### Request Headers

| Key | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Authorization | Bearer JWT_TOKEN |

### Success Response

``` json
{
  "_id": "665000000000000000000001",
  "name": "Laptop",
  "description": "Gaming Laptop",
  "price": 75000,
  "image": "https://example.com/laptop.jpg"
}

 ```

---

## 7\. Update Product

### Endpoint

``` http
PUT /api/products/:id

 ```

### Authorization

Bearer Token Required

### Request Headers

| Key | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Content-Type | application/json |
| Authorization | Bearer JWT_TOKEN |

### Request Body

``` json
{
  "name": "Updated Laptop",
  "description": "Updated Description",
  "price": 80000,
  "image": "https://example.com/new-image.jpg"
}

 ```

### Success Response

``` json
{
  "_id": "665000000000000000000001",
  "name": "Updated Laptop",
  "description": "Updated Description",
  "price": 80000,
  "image": "https://example.com/new-image.jpg"
}

 ```

---

## 8\. Delete Product

### Endpoint

``` http
DELETE /api/products/:id

 ```

### Authorization

Bearer Token Required

### Request Headers

| Key | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Authorization | Bearer JWT_TOKEN |

### Success Response

``` json
{
  "message": "Product removed"
}

 ```

---

# Admin APIs

Base Path:

``` txt
/api/v1

 ```

---

## 9\. Admin Access Route

### Endpoint

``` http
GET /api/v1/admin

 ```

### Description

Accessible only by admin users.

### Authorization

Bearer Token Required

### Request Headers

| Key | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Authorization | Bearer JWT_TOKEN |

### Success Response

``` txt
Admin Access

 ```

---

# Authentication Setup in Postman

## Step 1: Login

Call:

``` http
POST /api/v1/auth/login

 ```

Copy the returned JWT token.

---

## Step 2: Add Token

In Postman:

1. Open the request.
    
2. Go to the Authorization tab.
    
3. Select:
    

``` txt
Bearer Token

 ```

1. Paste the JWT token.
    

---

# Recommended Environment Variables

Create a Postman Environment:

| Variable | Value |
| --- | --- |
| BASE_URL | [http://localhost:5000](http://localhost:5000/) |
| TOKEN | JWT_TOKEN |

Example usage:

``` txt
{{BASE_URL}}/api/v1/auth/login

 ```

---

# Important Backend Notes

## Current API Prefix Inconsistency

The backend currently uses:

``` txt
/api/v1/auth

 ```

for authentication routes and:

``` txt
/api/products

 ```

for product routes.

This inconsistency can create frontend configuration issues.

Recommended improvement:

``` js
app.use("/api/v1/products", productRoutes);

 ```

Then all APIs will share the same versioned prefix:

``` txt
/api/v1/*

 ```

---

# Suggested Final API Structure

| Module | Route Prefix |
| --- | --- |
| Auth | /api/v1/auth |
| Products | /api/v1/products |
| Admin | /api/v1/admin |

---

# HTTP Status Codes

| Code | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| 200 | Success |
| 201 | Resource Created |
| 400 | Bad Request |
| 401 | Unauthorized |
| 403 | Forbidden |
| 404 | Not Found |
| 500 | Internal Server Error |

EndFragment
